title: Audit remaining authority-separation violations between mlb_model and sports_bot_v2
objective: Read-only audit. Identify every code location where sports_bot_v2 still contains local MLB decision logic, and every location where mlb_model still contains execution-style operational gating that belongs in sports_bot_v2. Produce a precise violation list with file, line range, and recommended disposition for each item. Do not change any code.
context: The intended architecture: mlb_model is the MLB recommendation authority. sports_bot_v2 is execution, risk, lifecycle, DB truth, and dashboard only. ALLOW_LOCAL_MLB_ORIGINATION=0 gates off the default local path, but local decision code still exists in sports_bot_v2 and some execution-style gates remain on the model side. This audit scopes exactly what remains before a cleanup task is written.
do_not_touch: All files are READ-ONLY in this task — no edits, no patches
inputs: Search for: local MLB decision logic, signal generation, origination paths, execution-style gates in model side
Cross-reference against ALLOW_LOCAL_MLB_ORIGINATION guard to understand what is gated vs what is dead code
deliverables: Violation list: for each item — file path, line range, description of what it does, whether it is gated/dead/active, recommended disposition (remove / move / keep-as-is)
Summary count: how many violations in sports_bot_v2, how many in mlb_model
Risk assessment: which items are safe to remove without a restart, which require a restart
Recommended follow-on task scope: what files and line ranges a cleanup task should be allowed to touch
acceptance_criteria: Every allowed file was read and searched
Violation list covers all five allowed files
Each violation entry has: file, line range, description, gated/dead/active classification, recommended disposition
verification: Confirm no files were modified (check that no write/edit operations were performed)
Confirm result file exists at correct outbox path
